briefdescription.para: Parameters for CONF_GetSettingsPath()
Creates a json with default experiment configuration block.

simplesect.para: json with default experiment configuration

briefdescription.para: Open all configuration files in plain text notebooks.

detaileddescription.para: Existing notebooks with the files are brought to the front.

briefdescription.para: Return a text wave with absolute paths to the JSON configuration files.

Automatically loads all *.json files from MIES Settings folder and opens and restores the corresponding windows Files are restored in case-insensitive alphanumeric order. Associated *_rig.json files are taken into account.
Returns a symbolic path to the settings folder.

simplesect.para: name of an igor symbolic path to the settings folder

briefdescription.para: Saves the GUI state of a window and all of its subwindows to a configuration file.

parameterdescription.para: file name of configuration file to save configuration

briefdescription.para: Restores the GUI state of window and all of its subwindows from a configuration file.

parameterdescription.para: file name of configuration file to read configuration
[optional, default = 0] if set to 1 then the panel type from the json is interpreted and a new panel of that type is opened
[optional, default = ""] name of secondary rig configuration file with complementary data. This parameter is valid when loading for a DA_Ephys panel

briefdescription.para: Saves the GUI state of a DA_Ephys panel to a configuration file.

parameterdescription.para: file name of configuration file to store DA_Ephys configuration

memberdef.argsstring: (variable jsonID, string fullFilePath, variable middleOfExperiment=defaultValue, variable forceNewPanel=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: CONF_RestoreDAEphys

briefdescription.para: Restores the GUI state of a DA_Ephys panel from a configuration file.

parameterdescription.para: json ID of json object to restore DA_Ephys panel from
full file path of the file where the json object was parsed from
[optional, default = 0] Allows MIES config in the middle of experiment. Instead of setting MCC parameters they are pulled from actively recording MCCs to configure MIES]

[optional, default = 0] When set opens always a fresh DA_Ephys panel, otherwise follows this priority order:
Reuses locked DA_Ephys panel with same device as saved in configuration
Uses open unlocked DA_Ephys panel
Opens new DA_Ephys panel

simplesect.para: name of the created DAEphys panel

briefdescription.para: Add the config file paths and SHA-256 hashes to the panel as user data.
Parses a json formatted string to a json object. This function shows a helpful error message if the parse fails.

simplesect.para: jsonID of the json object

briefdescription.para: Retrieves list of active headstages saved in a DA_Ephys configuration file.

simplesect.para: List of active head stages

briefdescription.para: Checks if EXPCONFIG_RESERVED_DATABLOCK exists in json; ASSERTion is thrown if not found.
Retrieves a string value from a setting.

simplesect.para: string from member with keyname in the EXPCONFIG_RESERVED_DATABLOCK

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a variable/boolean/null value from a saved control note: boolean control property values are also saved in the EXPCONFIG_FIELD_CTRLVVALUE field.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to off] allows to query optional entries, if the value could not be found this is returned instead

simplesect.para: value of the EXPCONFIG_FIELD_CTRLVVALUE field of the control

briefdescription.para: Returns the path to the first control named nicename found in the json in all saved windows This might as well be a ControlArray.

simplesect.para: Path to control in json, empty string if not found

briefdescription.para: Returns the path to the first control named nicename found traversing the json starting at basePath This might as well be a ControlArray. This function runs recursively.

simplesect.para: Path to control in json, empty string if not found

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a wave from a saved ControlArray It is REQUIRED that ALL ELEMENTS of the ControlArray are of NUMERIC type.

simplesect.para: text wave with data from ControlArray

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a string value from a saved control.

simplesect.para: value of the EXPCONFIG_FIELD_CTRLSVALUE field of the control

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a variable value from a saved control.

simplesect.para: value of the EXPCONFIG_FIELD_CTRLVVALUE field of the control

briefdescription.para: Returns a wave with all configuration sections ( aka control groups). This equals the top level keys without the EXPCONFIG_RESERVED_DATABLOCK key.

simplesect.para: Text wave with all named configuration sections

briefdescription.para: Returns a two column free wave with ControlArrayName and associated control list from a window.

simplesect.para: Text wave with two columns and in each row ControlArray name (column ARRAYNAME) and control list (column CTRLNAMELIST)

briefdescription.para: Gathers recursively all control nice names and control paths from a configuration json by traversing all sub objects.

parameterdescription.para: ctrlData wave, 2d four column text wave as created in CONF_JSONToWindow(). This wave is updated by this function.

briefdescription.para: Restores GUI state of a window from a json The following userdata properties are considered: EXPCONFIG_UDATA_EXCLUDE_RESTORE, EXPCONFIG_UDATA_RESTORE_PRIORITY It is supported that the current main reference window in the GUI changes its name. It is not supported that a not yet restored subwindow of it changes its name.

parameterdescription.para: main reference window name in GUI
Bit mask which properties are restored from WindowControlSavingMask constants

simplesect.para: name of main window after restore

briefdescription.para: Returns the window with the set window handle.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, default = EXPCONFIG_UDATA_WINHANDLE] userdata key that stores the handle value

simplesect.para: Window name of the window with the given handle; empty string if not found.

memberdef.argsstring: (string wName, variable restoreMask, variable jsonID, string ctrlName=defaultValue, string jsonPath=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: CONF_RestoreControl

briefdescription.para: Restores properties of a control from a json The following control userdata properties are considered: EXPCONFIG_UDATA_EXCLUDE_RESTORE; EXPCONFIG_UDATA_BUTTONPRESS (for Buttons)

parameterdescription.para: Bit mask which properties are restored from WindowControlSavingMask constants

[optional, default = n/a] When given: the control is expected to be a named json object (with the control nice name) If not given: the jsons second level (assuming default format) is searched for the associated object. This is slower.

briefdescription.para: Serializes all controls of a window and its subwindows into a json object.

parameterdescription.para: bit pattern based configuration setting for saving

simplesect.para: WindowControlSavingMask

parameterdescription.para: [optional, default = ""], list of control type codes for excluded control types for saving e.g. "1;6;" to exclude all buttons and charts

simplesect.para: json ID of object where all controls where serialized into

briefdescription.para: Saves complete GUI state of a window in a json For coupled CheckBoxes aka (radio buttons) the enabled radio button is saved, the disabled is not saved. For three or more coupled CheckBoxes the first enabled radio button is saved, the disabled are not saved. For three or more coupled CheckBoxes an assertion is thrown if no CheckBox is set.

parameterdescription.para: Bit mask which properties are saved from WindowControlSavingMask constants
[optional, default = ""] List of excluded control types that are ignored

simplesect.para: jsonID ID of json containing the serialized GUI data

memberdef.argsstring: (string wName, string ctrlName, variable saveMask, variable jsonID, string excCtrlTypes, string excUserKeys)
memberdef.name: CONF_ControlToJSON

briefdescription.para: Adds properties of a control to a json.

parameterdescription.para: Bit mask which properties are saved from WindowControlSavingMask constants
List of excluded control types that are ignored
List of excluded keys of userdata fields that are ignored

briefdescription.para: Opens MCCs and restores Headstage Association from configuration data to DA_Ephys panel.

parameterdescription.para: ID of json object with configuration data
middle of experiment - uploads MCC relevant settings from panel to MCC instead

briefdescription.para: Retrieves current User Pressure settings to json.

simplesect.para: jsonID ID of json object with user pressure configuration data

briefdescription.para: Restore User Pressure settings.

parameterdescription.para: ID of json object with configuration data

briefdescription.para: Retrieves current amplifier and pressure settings to json.

simplesect.para: jsonID ID of json object with user pressure configuration data

memberdef.argsstring: (string device, variable headStage, variable jsonID, string basePath)
memberdef.name: CONF_RestoreAmplifierSettings

briefdescription.para: Restore the per headstage amplifier settings.

parameterdescription.para: MIES headstage number, must be in the range [0, NUM_HEADSTAGES]
ID of json object with configuration data
absolute path in the json file to search the entries

briefdescription.para: Find the list index of a connected amplifier serial number.

parameterdescription.para: Amplifier Serial Number to search for

briefdescription.para: Position MCC windows to upper right monitor using nircmd.exe.

parameterdescription.para: One of 4 monitors to position MCCs in

briefdescription.para: Loads, parses and joins a *_rig.json file to a main configuration file.

parameterdescription.para: full file path of rig file

briefdescription.para: CONF Import user settings to configure paramters for Ephys experiments

detaileddescription.para: The Configuration module allows to save and load the GUI state of complete windows including its subwindows

The main functions are CONF_SaveWindow and CONF_RestoreWindow. CONF_SaveWindow automatically detects if the current window is a DA_Ephys panel and uses the DA_Ephys GUI state savig configuration settings and saves additional information. CONF_RestoreWindow also detects if the current window is of DA_Ephys type and handles that specific for this case. CONF_RestoreDAEphys can be called directly as well if there is no panel open. It will open a new DA_Ephys panel automatically if no other viable candidate panel was found.

The GUI state is saved in a text file in json format. In the basic structure of the file the main blocks are the windows and subwindows and within are the control blocks. A bitmask parameter allows to configure which control information is saved. See WindowControlSavingMask Currently supported is Value, Position/Size, Userdata, Disabled state and Type of a control. Not implemented is e.g. Color/Background Color As Value only the V_Value and/or S_Value and/or S_DataFolder is saved. Special control properties for e.g. ListBox like V_SelCol are currently not implemented.

How GUI controls are handled can be controlled through userdata settings of each control. The following userdata settings are supported:
Config_NiceName: sets a human readable name of the control used in the configuration file This name may not be a duplicate of another controls name or ControlArray name. Also suffixing with " ControlGroup" is not allowed.
Config_GroupPath: Sets a group path in which the control appears in the configuration file. If not set then "Generic" is used. The group path elements can not contain the '/' character. Several levels can be separated by ";", e.g. "level1;level2;". In the configuration file the group path elements are suffixed by " ControlGroup" which identifies them as path compared to actual control names.
Config_DontSave: When set to 1 the control is ignored on saving the window.
Config_DontRestore: When set to 1 the control is ignored on restoring the window.
Config_PushButtonOnRestore: Only valid with Button controls. When the button is restored a button press is issued.
Config_RestorePriority: Sets a double precision number that defines the order in which controls get restored. Lower numbers correspond to earlier restore. If not set a priority of Inf is used. If multiple controls have the same priority the order of restore is not defined.
Config_RadioCouplingFunc: A function name of a function that returns a text wave with lists of coupled CheckBoxes (aka RadioButtons). It is assumed that only one CheckBox of the group is in enabled state at the same time. See DAP_GetRadioButtonCoupling for example.
ControlArray: Stores a name of a group of controls. All controls with the same ControlArray name are saved as group with that name in the configuration file. ControlArrays save only values and nothing is saved if the configured WindowControlSavingMask does not include values.
ControlArrayIndex: Only valid if also ControlArray is set. Stores the index number of the control in its ControlArray. All member of the same ControlArray must have different indices. Currently not more than 100 controls are allowed for each ControlArray.
The function WindowtoJSON allows to give optional a list of control types that should be excluded.
If the type of the control was saved, it is checked against the GUI controls type on restore.
CheckBoxes are saved with true/false values.
For the DA_Ephys panel: The function CONF_DefaultSettings sets default values for parameters not present in the GUI.

Special mask flags:
EXPCONFIG_SAVE_ONLY_RELEVANT: Saves only most relevant value data of a control. It is configured in EXPCONFIG_GUI_PREFERRED
EXPCONFIG_SAVE_POPUPMENU_AS_STRING_ONLY: Saves value for PopupMenu only as string
EXPCONFIG_SAVE_POPUPMENU_AS_INDEX_ONLY: Saves value for PopupMenu only as index value
EXPCONFIG_MINIMIZE_ON_RESTORE: Minimize window while restoring it.
EXPCONFIG_SAVE_BUTTONS_ONLY_PRESSED: Saves Buttons only when its userdata Config_PushButtonOnRestore is "1"

Handling of window names on restore: The currently selected windows main window is used as reference. The main window name in the configuration file is internally replaced with the current main window. This allows to save from "DataBrowser" and load to "DataBrowser2".

Handling of errors in input data: If input data is of unexpected format an ASSERTion or RTE is issued. The wrapping function converts the RTE to an ASSERT. The panel restore is aborted and the panel stays in its state. It gets unhided.
Default configuration for generic windows: By default only the Value property is saved and controls from EXPCONFIG_EXCLUDE_CTRLTYPES excluded.

Default configuration for DA_Ephys panel:
By default only the Value property is saved
Controls from DAEPHYS_EXCLUDE_CTRLTYPES are excluded.
The window is minimized while restoring
PopupMenu values are saved as string only
Buttons are only saved if its userdata Config_PushButtonOnRestore is "1"
Only most relevant data of a control is saved.
